root_cause: hyperliquid.__init__.py (v0.4.66) executes sys.modules['ccxt'] = hyperliquid.ccxt, overwriting the real third-party CCXT with a minimal shim that lacks exchange classes like binance, okx, bybit

reproduced_import_orders.ccxt-first: import ccxt → import hyperliquid → bare import ccxt returns hyperliquid.ccxt (FAIL expected → resolver fixes this)
reproduced_import_orders.hyperliquid-first: import hyperliquid → bare import ccxt returns hyperliquid.ccxt (FAIL expected → resolver fixes this)
reproduced_import_orders.alternating: Both adapters imported → ccxt is real (PASS)

resolver_design: 3-strategy cascade: 1) sys.modules check, 2) fresh import, 3) clear hyperliquid.ccxt shadow keys then re-import. All encapsulated in import_resolver.py. Callers never need to manipulate sys.modules.

public_live_probe.ccxt_module_name_verified: ccxt (real) — never hyperliquid.ccxt during any probe

remaining_upstream_risks: hyperliquid.ccxt shadowing is intentional SDK behavior; will need update if SDK changes shadow mechanism
Resolver assumes ccxt distribution is installed via pip; air-gapped installations may need path override
Windows O_EXCL behavior differs from POSIX for FileLock (not related to import isolation, noted for awareness)
